axis: bridge / JSONL alert trend analyzer — schema-evolution-tolerant counterpart to bridge-22 over the JSONL sibling
title: tool/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend.hexa — read-only trend analyzer over state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl with extra_field_distribution + scope_distribution
summary: ω-bridge-22 (commit 7d8fc8fa) shipped tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa over the TSV history. ω-bridge-23 (commit 8e145470) added a JSONL sibling at state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl with the same per-rule rows under a `scope` discriminator. This cycle adds tool/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend.hexa — a sweeper-safe .hexa analyzer that consumes the JSONL sibling for downstream tooling that prefers JSON over TSV, with two JSONL-only conveniences enabled by the richer payload format: (7) extra_field_distribution — per-rule histogram of the `extra` field values (e.g. consecutive_warn fires K=2 mostly, but K=5 spikes weekly); and (8) scope_distribution — count rows by scope (forward-compat for future cycles emitting other scopes into the same JSONL beyond atlas_alert). Schema-evolution tolerance: rows with ADDITIONAL fields beyond the documented schema are accepted; rows MISSING expected fields default safely (rule='unknown', etc). Sentinel: __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ <PASS|INSUFFICIENT|EMPTY|SKIP> total=N last_window=W top_rule=R fire_rate_per_hour=X. CLI: --history PATH / --window-hours H / --filter-scope NAME / --json / --quiet / --help. 8 fixture cases validated.

predecessors.omega_bridge_17: built tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED sentinels for warn_ratio + insufficient_ratio rules
predecessors.omega_bridge_19: added consecutive_warn (streak) and recent_warn_ratio (time window) rules with rule-specific extra= fields (streak=K / window_minutes=M)
predecessors.omega_bridge_21: extended subscriber to persist FIRED outcomes to state/atlas_alert_history.tsv (TAB-separated, append-only, 7-column schema)
predecessors.omega_bridge_22: tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa — analyzer over the TSV history with 6 metrics (alert_count_total, alert_count_last_window, per_rule_count, mtba_per_rule, top_rule_in_window, fire_rate_per_hour); commit 7d8fc8fa
predecessors.omega_bridge_23_24: (commit 8e145470) — Part A added JSONL sibling state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl with `scope=atlas_alert` discriminator and rule-specific extra= fields preserved per row; Part B added per-rule cooldown dedup (orthogonal to bridge-25)

context.problem: The JSONL sibling exists for downstream consumers that prefer one-object-per-line JSON over TSV (richer payloads, easier schema evolution, friendlier to jq / Python / shell-line pipelines), but no tool reasons over the JSONL. ω-bridge-22's analyzer is hardcoded to TSV and split('\t'); reusing it would require a TSV-shaped projection (loss of fidelity on extra= field structure). Worse, the TSV analyzer would break the moment a future cycle adds an 8th column or a new rule field — the JSONL format was specifically chosen to absorb this kind of evolution without parser breakage.

design_alternatives_considered.option: A — extend tool/atlas_alert_trend.hexa with a --jsonl flag
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: the two parsers share little code (TSV split is field-positional; JSONL parse is keyed) and the verdict surface mostly overlaps but the metrics extra_field_distribution + scope_distribution are JSONL-specific. Mixing them in one tool means a TSV regression could break JSONL or vice versa, and the sentinel name would have to be conditional. Producer/consumer split honored by ω-bridge-22 deserves the same split here.

design_alternatives_considered.option: B — separate sweeper-safe .hexa tool over the JSONL with its own sentinel (CHOSEN)
design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: matches the bridge-22 precedent exactly (read-only consumer, single-purpose, sweeper-safe, distinct sentinel surface __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__). Lets the two analyzers evolve independently — bridge-22 stays simple over TSV, bridge-25 owns the schema-evolution surface for JSONL. Adds JSONL-only metrics that the TSV format can't cleanly express.

design_alternatives_considered.option: C — replace bridge-22 with a JSONL-only analyzer and deprecate the TSV path
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: the TSV is still the canonical 'simple to grep / awk' surface; bridge-21 force-added it as schema stub and operators may have shell habits around it. ω-bridge-23 deliberately made the JSONL a SIBLING (not replacement) with independent failure handling — the analyzer surface should mirror that.

design_alternatives_considered.option: D — pure-hexa JSON parser (no python shell-out)
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: hexa lacks a stdlib json.loads (only ad-hoc parse_int_field-style extractors used in lhc_opendata_bridge.hexa). Hand-rolling JSON tokenization for nested escapes / unicode would be 100+ lines of fragile code that python solves in 1 line (json.loads). bridge-22 already established the single-shell-out pattern (one python3 call) for time math; same pattern extends to JSON parse.

files_created.purpose: read-only trend analyzer over state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl with schema-evolution tolerance and JSONL-specific metrics
structural_blocks: header (raw-9, raw-71, raw-77 compliance + sentinel doc + usage examples)
pre-flight: SKIP if file missing (exit 0, sentinel emitted)
raw-content load (read_file) → /tmp/<ts>.jsonl scratch (avoids shell-quoting JSON content)

single python3 shell-out: json.loads per line, schema-evolution defaults, scope filter, computes TOTAL/MALFORMED/MISSING_RECOVERED/SCOPE/VERDICT/RULE/WIN_*/SPAN_HOURS/FIRE_RATE/MTBA/EXTRA structured key=value lines
early-exit on EMPTY / INSUFFICIENT verdicts (with scope_distribution emitted in EMPTY case for forward-compat visibility)
PASS path: human-readable summary (suppressed when --quiet OR --json), warn forwarding for malformed lines
JSON output (when --json): nested extra_field_distribution {rule: {extra_value: count}} + flat scope_distribution {scope: count}
sentinel emission (always last): __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ <verdict> total=N last_window=W top_rule=R fire_rate_per_hour=X

jsonl_row_shape.scope: atlas_alert (default; future cycles may emit other scopes into the same file)
jsonl_row_shape.ratio: float (0.0 if not applicable, e.g. consecutive_warn)
jsonl_row_shape.extra: string — empty for warn_ratio/insufficient_ratio; 'streak=K' for consecutive_warn; 'window_minutes=M' for recent_warn_ratio; future rules may add new shapes

schema_consumed.schema_evolution_policy: extra fields ignored (forward-compat); missing fields default to safe sentinels (rule='unknown', scope='unknown', extra=''); rows with bad JSON or non-object payload counted as malformed and skipped without aborting the analysis

metrics_emitted.name: alert_count_last_window
metrics_emitted.definition: scope-matched rows whose ts falls within [now - window_hours, now]

metrics_emitted.name: per_rule_count
metrics_emitted.definition: histogram of rule firings over file lifetime (scope-matched)

metrics_emitted.name: mtba_per_rule
metrics_emitted.definition: mean time between alerts in seconds, per rule (NA if rule has <2 fires)

metrics_emitted.name: top_rule_in_window
metrics_emitted.definition: most-fired rule within window_hours; sentinel 'none' if window empty; ties broken by sorted-name first-encountered

metrics_emitted.name: fire_rate_per_hour
metrics_emitted.definition: total / span_hours, where span_hours = (last_ts - first_ts) / 3600; 0.0 if all rows share the same second

metrics_emitted.name: extra_field_distribution
metrics_emitted.definition: (NEW for bridge-25) per-rule histogram of non-empty `extra` field values; lets ops see e.g. consecutive_warn fires streak=2 mostly but streak=5 spikes; empty-extra rows omitted from this surface (no signal)
metrics_emitted.where: summary `extra_field_distribution:` block, JSON `extra_field_distribution` nested object {rule: {extra_value: count}}

metrics_emitted.name: scope_distribution
metrics_emitted.definition: (NEW for bridge-25) histogram of scopes seen across ALL rows pre-filter; surfaces forward-compat insight when the JSONL accumulates rows from cycles other than atlas_alert; emitted in EMPTY verdict too (so operators see why their scope filter zeroed)
metrics_emitted.where: summary `scope_distribution:` block, JSON `scope_distribution` flat object {scope: count}

flags: --json                emit one-line JSON object before sentinel; suppresses human summary
--filter-scope NAME   only count rows with this scope (default atlas_alert; scope_distribution still shows pre-filter counts)
sentinels: __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ INSUFFICIENT total=N last_window=0 top_rule=none fire_rate_per_hour=0.0000  (when scope-matched <3)
__ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ EMPTY        total=0 last_window=0 top_rule=none fire_rate_per_hour=0.0000  (no scope-matched data rows)

compliance.raw_9_hexa_only_suffix: new file is tool/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend.hexa (.hexa suffix; sweeper-safe)
compliance.raw_71_report_only: tool reads the history JSONL and parses; never writes; uses /tmp/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend_in_<ts>.jsonl as scratch (cleaned up post-shell-out)
compliance.raw_73_admissibility: verdict surface mirrors network-3 deferred-recommendation pattern (INSUFFICIENT for sub-quorum data); SKIP for missing file (exit 0); PASS for >=3 scope-matched rows
compliance.raw_77_append_only: JSONL consumed read-only; producer contract from ω-bridge-23 preserved (scope=atlas_alert discriminator + JSON-per-line); no writes, no rewrites; concurrent appender tolerated (read_file is single fs syscall snapshot)
compliance.raw_77_state_alert_history_jsonl_readonl: explicitly READ-ONLY on state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl per task constraint
compliance.raw_80_sentinel: __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ added (PASS / INSUFFICIENT / EMPTY / SKIP shapes); independent of __ALERT_TREND__ (TSV analyzer) and __ATLAS_ALERT__ (producer)
compliance.no_parallel_session_collision: added only tool/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend.hexa; did NOT touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own — verified via git status --short before staging
compliance.explicit_path_commit: git add tool/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend.hexa only — no `git add -A`
compliance.no_anima_commit: closure JSON written to /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_bridge_25_alert_jsonl_trend_analyzer.json — UNTRACKED, no git operations in anima
compliance.history_ops_pinned_sha: no history rewrite; predecessor SHAs pinned (bridge-22=7d8fc8fa, bridge-23+24=8e145470, current HEAD nexus=8e145470 at task start, pinned-or-later met)
compliance.hexa_interp_direct: validation runs invoked /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp directly (with HEXA_LOCAL=1 to bypass the darwin landing routing); not the /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa wrapper

fixtures.name: fixture_a — typical 10 rows mixed rules over 3-day span
fixtures.expected: PASS verdict, total=10, span≈67h, fire_rate≈0.15, per-rule counts {warn_ratio:5, insufficient_ratio:2, consecutive_warn:2, recent_warn_ratio:1}, MTBA per rule populated for rules with ≥2 fires, extra_field_distribution surfaces consecutive_warn streak values + recent_warn_ratio window_minutes
fixtures.observed: __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ PASS total=10 last_window=10 top_rule=warn_ratio fire_rate_per_hour=0.1481; per_rule_count={warn_ratio:5, insufficient_ratio:2, consecutive_warn:2, recent_warn_ratio:1}; MTBA all match; extra_field_distribution shows consecutive_warn::streak=2, consecutive_warn::streak=3, recent_warn_ratio::window_minutes=15

fixtures.name: fixture_d — missing path
fixtures.expected: SKIP verdict, exit 0, no metric output

fixtures.name: fixture_e — 5 valid + 2 malformed JSON (1 plain text, 1 unclosed brace)
fixtures.expected: PASS verdict, total=5, malformed=2, both bad lines warned
fixtures.observed: warn: malformed JSON skipped: this is not json at all; warn: malformed JSON skipped: {"this is": "broken json with no closing; __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ PASS total=5 last_window=5 top_rule=warn_ratio fire_rate_per_hour=1.2500; malformed=2

fixtures.name: fixture_f — 5 rows all consecutive_warn with extra=streak=2/3/2/2/5
fixtures.expected: PASS verdict, extra_field_distribution shows {streak=2: 3, streak=3: 1, streak=5: 1}
fixtures.observed: __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ PASS total=5 last_window=5 top_rule=consecutive_warn fire_rate_per_hour=1.2500; extra_field_distribution {consecutive_warn::streak=2 = 3, consecutive_warn::streak=3 = 1, consecutive_warn::streak=5 = 1} — exact match to spec

fixtures.name: fixture_g — mixed scope (3 atlas_alert + 2 future_unknown); test default + --filter-scope future_unknown
fixtures.expected: default scope filter counts 3 atlas_alert rows (PASS); --filter-scope future_unknown counts only the 2 future rows (INSUFFICIENT since <3); scope_distribution shows both pre-filter counts
fixtures.observed: default: PASS total=3 last_window=3 top_rule=warn_ratio; scope_distribution {atlas_alert:3, future_unknown:2}. --filter-scope future_unknown: INSUFFICIENT total=2 last_window=0 top_rule=none fire_rate_per_hour=0.0000

fixtures.name: fixture_h — rows with extra unknown JSON fields (severity, host, trace_id, operator)
fixtures.expected: parser ignores unknowns, doesn't crash; valid rows counted normally
fixtures.observed: PASS total=4 last_window=4 top_rule=warn_ratio fire_rate_per_hour=1.3333; per_rule_count and MTBA correct; extra_field_distribution surfaces consecutive_warn::streak=2 only (other rows have empty extra)

additional_smoke.name: --json mode on fixture_f produces valid JSON parseable by python3 -m json.tool
additional_smoke.expected: single-line valid JSON before sentinel; nested extra_field_distribution; flat scope_distribution
additional_smoke.observed: JSON validates; nested {"consecutive_warn":{"streak=2":3,"streak=3":1,"streak=5":1}} renders correctly; sentinel follows on its own line

additional_smoke.name: default invocation against live state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl (not yet emitted in production)
additional_smoke.expected: SKIP verdict (file does not yet exist; bridge-23 producer will create on first FIRED event)

additional_smoke.name: --help mode
additional_smoke.observed: all 5 flags documented + 4 verdicts listed; exits without sentinel emission

edge_cases_handled.schema_evolution_extra_fields: rows with unknown additional JSON fields (severity, host, trace_id, etc) are silently accepted — only ts/scope/rule/extra are read; future cycles can extend the row shape without breaking this parser
edge_cases_handled.schema_evolution_missing_fields: rows missing ts → counted as malformed (cannot place on timeline); rows missing rule/scope → defaulted to 'unknown' and missing_field_recovered counter incremented (forward-compat soft landing rather than hard reject)
edge_cases_handled.malformed_json_lines: lines that fail json.loads are counted in malformed and printed as warnings (truncated to 120 chars) on stderr; analyzer continues with remaining valid rows
edge_cases_handled.non_object_json: JSON that parses but isn't a dict (e.g. arrays, strings, numbers as top-level) counted as malformed and skipped
edge_cases_handled.empty_lines: blank lines silently skipped (no warning, no malformed count)
edge_cases_handled.scope_filter_default_atlas_alert: default scope=atlas_alert protects against bridge-23's discriminator pattern being hijacked by future cycles emitting other scopes into the same file
edge_cases_handled.scope_distribution_pre_filter: scope_distribution is computed BEFORE the scope filter, so operators see the full picture (e.g. 'I filtered for atlas_alert and got 0 rows but the file has 50 rows scoped future_unknown')
edge_cases_handled.extra_field_dist_skips_empty: rows with extra='' do not appear in extra_field_distribution surface (no signal in 'all-empty for this rule' — would be noise); rules with all-empty extra show '(no rules with non-empty extra)' in human summary
edge_cases_handled.single_fire_per_rule_mtba_NA: MTBA requires ≥2 fires for a rule; rules with exactly 1 fire emit MTBA=NA in summary and null in JSON — same as bridge-22
edge_cases_handled.single_second_span_zero_fire_rate: if first_ts == last_ts (all rows same second), span_hours=0 → fire_rate_per_hour=0.0 (avoids div-by-zero)
edge_cases_handled.future_dated_rows_excluded_from_window: python computes cutoff = now - window_hours; rows with t > now or t < cutoff excluded from win_total / top_rule (defensive against clock skew)
edge_cases_handled.verdict_stratification: SKIP > EMPTY > INSUFFICIENT > PASS; each early-returns with its own sentinel; EMPTY case still emits scope_distribution (helps diagnose 'why did my filter zero')
edge_cases_handled.json_quiet_interaction: --quiet suppresses human summary AND warning forwarding; --json suppresses human summary; sentinel emits last in all modes (raw 80)
edge_cases_handled.tmp_file_cleanup: scratch /tmp/atlas_alert_jsonl_trend_in_<ts>.jsonl rm -f'd after python shell-out (best-effort, errors suppressed)
edge_cases_handled.raw_content_isolated_from_shell: JSONL content can contain arbitrary quote/backslash patterns; we write it to a tmp file rather than embedding in the python -c heredoc, so shell quoting cannot corrupt the input

operational_notes.default_invocation_today: live state/atlas_alert_history.jsonl does not yet exist (bridge-23 producer creates on first FIRED event; no firings in production yet); analyzer emits SKIP — expected. PASS verdict will surface as the bridge cluster accumulates firings.
operational_notes.expected_steady_state: with bridge-24's per-rule cooldown dedup, JSONL accretion is even slower than TSV-only emission used to be; analyzer cost is dominated by the single python3 shell-out (~50ms cold) and is cheap to run in any sweep loop
operational_notes.filter_scope_evolution: if a future cycle reuses the JSONL with scope='other_thing', bridge-25 default invocation (--filter-scope atlas_alert) ignores them by design; ops can run with --filter-scope other_thing to get a separate trend view; scope_distribution surfaces both
operational_notes.back_compat: no edits to existing tools or files; pure addition; existing __ALERT_TREND__ (bridge-22), __ATLAS_ALERT__ (bridge-17/19), and __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ surfaces unchanged
operational_notes.no_history_growth: tool is read-only; does not contribute to any state file growth

future_candidates: tool/atlas_alert_dual_trend.hexa — meta-analyzer that reads both __ALERT_TREND__ and __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ sentinels and reconciles them (TSV vs JSONL drift detection — would catch a producer bug where one sibling is updated but not the other)
extra_field_anomaly: detect when extra_field_distribution histogram shifts (e.g. consecutive_warn used to fire streak=2 90% of the time but suddenly streak=5 dominates → escalation signal)
wire __ALERT_JSONL_TREND__ into the ω-network cross-repo dashboard alongside __ALERT_TREND__ (same row, two columns: TSV vs JSONL)
tool/atlas_alert_jsonl_to_parquet.hexa — when the JSONL grows beyond N rows, materialize a parquet file for efficient long-window analytics (analyzer would gain --parquet flag to read either)
scope_aware fire_rate: compute fire_rate per scope (currently fire_rate uses scope-filtered total; could surface per-scope rates simultaneously)
rotation hook: paired with bridge-22 follow-up — when JSONL exceeds size threshold, archive to state/atlas_alert_history_<yyyymm>.jsonl and rotate; --history-glob flag to read across rotated files
